subject: Chemistry
title: The Hindenburg Disaster
description: A 60-second lesson on The Hindenburg Disaster.

hook.text: On May 6, 1937, the Hindenburg airship burst into flames and crashed in 34 seconds. 36 people died.

buildup.text: The Hindenburg was filled with hydrogen—the lightest and most flammable gas in the universe.

discovery.text: Hydrogen burns with oxygen explosively: 2H₂ + O₂ → 2H₂O. The product is just water.

twist.text: Germany used hydrogen because the US refused to sell them helium. Politics chose the dangerous gas.

climax.text: The disaster ended the airship era overnight. Heavier-than-air planes took over.

punchline.text: Hydrogen plus oxygen equals water—and an inferno.

quiz.question: What is produced when hydrogen burns in oxygen?
